name: picpro

metadata.name: picpro
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 (GPLv2)
metadata.summary: picpro a kitsrus PIC CLI programmer

documentation:
# picpro a kitsrus PIC CLI programmer

This is complete rewrite of kitsrus_pic_programmer into Python 3 + bunch of fixes and features.

[![Tox tests](https://github.com/Salamek/picpro/actions/workflows/python-test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Salamek/picpro/actions/workflows/python-test.yml)

## Supported kitsrus programmers
    * K128
    * K149-A
    * K149-B
    * K150 (Tested)
    
See http://www.kitsrus.com/pic.html
    

## Installation

### PIP (pip3 on some distros)

```
pip install picpro
```

### Repository
You can also use these repositories maintained by me
#### Debian and derivatives

Add repository by running these commands

```
$ wget -O- https://repository.salamek.cz/deb/salamek.gpg | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/salamek-archive-keyring.gpg
$ echo "deb     [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/salamek-archive-keyring.gpg] https://repository.salamek.cz/deb/pub all main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/salamek.cz.list
```

And then you can install a package picpro

```
$ apt update && apt install picpro
```

#### Archlinux

Add repository by adding this at end of file /etc/pacman.conf

```
[salamek]
Server = https://repository.salamek.cz/arch/pub/any
SigLevel = Optional
```

and then install by running

```
$ pacman -Sy picpro
```


## Usage

```
Command details:
    program             Program PIC chip.
    verify              Verify PIC flash.
    dump                Dump PIC data as binary.

Usage:
    picpro program -p PORT -i HEX_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--id=PIC_ID] [--fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE...] [--icsp]
    picpro verify -p PORT -i HEX_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--icsp]
    picpro dump <mem_type> -p PORT -b BIN_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--icsp]
    picpro (-h | --help)


Options:
    --icsp                           Enable ISCP programming.
    --fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE      Set fuse value directly.
    --id=PIC_ID                      Set PIC id to be programmed in pic.
    -p PORT --port=PORT              Set serial port where programmer is connected.
    -t PIC_TYPE --pic_type=PIC_TYPE  Pic type you are programming/reading.
    -i HEX_FILE --hex_file=HEX_FILE  Hex file to flash or to read.
    -b BIN_FILE --bin_file=BIN_FILE  Bin file path.

```

### Program chip

```bash
picpro program -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -i YOUR_HEX_FILE.hex -t 12F675
```

### Verify chip program

```bash
picpro verify -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -i YOUR_HEX_FILE.hex -t 12F675
```

### Dump ROM as bin file

```bash
picpro dump rom -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -b dump.rom -t 12F675
```

### Dump EEPROM as bin file

```bash
picpro dump eeprom -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -b dump.eeprom -t 12F675
```

### Program chip via ISCP

```bash
picpro program -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -i YOUR_HEX_FILE.hex -t 12F675 --icsp
```

### Program chip and override fuses provided from HEX file

```bash
picpro program -p /dev/ttyUSB0 -i YOUR_HEX_FILE.hex -t 12F675 --fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE --fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE
```

Chip info entry interface

functions.name: picpro.tools.indexwise_and
documentation:
Given a list of fuse values, and a list of (index, value) pairs,
return a list x such that x[index] = fuses[index] & value.

functions.name: picpro.tools.swab_record
documentation: Given a record from a hex file, return a new copy with adjacent data bytes swapped.

functions.name: picpro.tools.range_filter_records
documentation: Given a list of HEX file records, return a new list of HEX file records containing only the HEX data within the specified address range.

functions.name: picpro.tools.merge_records
documentation: Given a list of HEX file records and a data buffer with its own base address (default=0), merge the HEX file records into a new copy of the data buffer.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.FormatError
documentation: Indicates an error in the chipinfo file's format.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.FuseError
documentation: Indicates an erroneous fuse value.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.InvalidResponseError
documentation: Indicates that device did not return the expected response.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.InvalidCommandSequenceError
documentation: Indicates commands executed in improper order.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.InvalidValueError
documentation: Indicates incorrect value given for command argument.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.InvalidRecordError
documentation: Indicates an improperly formatted HEX record.

classes.name: picpro.exceptions.InvalidChecksumError
documentation: Indicates a failed checksum test.

classes.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface._expect
documentation: Raise an exception if the expected response byte is not sent by the PIC programmer before timeout.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.reset
documentation: Resets the PIC Programmer's on-board controller.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.echo
documentation:
Instructs the PIC programmer to echo back the message
string.  Returns the PIC programmer's response.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.init_programming_vars
documentation:
Inform PIC programmer of general parameters of PIC to be
programmed.  Necessary for use of various other commands.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface._set_programming_voltages_command
documentation: Turn the PIC programming voltages on or off.  Must be called as part of other commands which read or write PIC data.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_rom
documentation: Write data to ROM.  data should be a binary string of data, high byte first.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_eeprom
documentation: Write data to EEPROM.  Data size must be small enough to fit in EEPROM.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_id_fuses
documentation:
Program PIC ID and fuses.  For 16-bit processors, fuse values
are not committed until program_18fxxxx_fuse() is called.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_calibration
documentation:
Program calibration
Args:
    calibrate:
    fuse:

Returns:

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.read_rom
documentation: Returns contents of PIC ROM as a string of big-endian values.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.read_eeprom
documentation: Returns data stored in PIC EEPROM.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.read_config
documentation: Reads chip ID and programmed ID, fuses, and calibration.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.erase_chip
documentation: Erases all data from chip.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.rom_is_blank
documentation: Returns True if PIC ROM is blank.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.eeprom_is_blank
documentation: Returns True if PIC EEPROM is blank.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_18fxxxx_fuse
documentation: Commits fuse values previously loaded using program_id_fuses()

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.wait_until_chip_in_socket
documentation: Blocks until a chip is inserted in the programming socket.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.wait_until_chip_out_of_socket
documentation: Blocks until chip is removed from programming socket.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.programmer_firmware_version
documentation: Returns the PIC programmer's numeric firmware version.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.programmer_protocol
documentation: Returns the PIC programmer's protocol version in text form.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.program_debug_vector
documentation: Sets the PIC's debugging vector.

methods.name: picpro.ProtocolInterface.ProtocolInterface.read_debug_vector
documentation: Returns the value of the PIC's debugging vector.
A convenient interface to the DIY serial/USB PIC programmer kits

modules.name: picpro.bin.picpro
documentation:
Main entry-point into the 'pic_k150_programmer' CLI application.

The picpro3 provides a simple program-level and command-line-level interface to the PIC programmers made by kitsrus.com. (K150 and compatible)

License: GPL2
Website: https://gitlab.salamek.cz/sadam/pic_k150_programmer.git

Command details:
    program             Program PIC chip.
    verify              Verify PIC flash.
    dump                Dump PIC data as binary.
    erase               Erase PIC.
    chipinfo            Prints chipinfo as JSON in terminal.
    hexinfo             Prints information about hexfile.

Usage:
    picpro program -p PORT -i HEX_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--id=PIC_ID] [--fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE...] [--icsp]
    picpro verify -p PORT -i HEX_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--icsp]
    picpro erase -p PORT -t PIC_TYPE [--icsp]
    picpro dump <mem_type> -p PORT -o HEX_FILE -t PIC_TYPE [--icsp]
    picpro chipinfo [<PIC_TYPE>]
    picpro hexinfo <HEX_FILE> <PIC_TYPE>
    picpro (-h | --help)


Options:
    --icsp                           Enable ISCP programming.
    --fuse=FUSE_NAME:FUSE_VALUE      Set fuse value directly.
    --id=PIC_ID                      Set PIC id to be programmed in pic.
    -p PORT --port=PORT              Set serial port where programmer is connected.
    -t PIC_TYPE --pic_type=PIC_TYPE  Pic type you are programming/reading.
    -i HEX_FILE --hex_file=HEX_FILE  Hex file to flash or to read.
    -o HEX_FILE --hex_file=HEX_FILE  Hex file to write.
!/usr/bin/python

functions.name: picpro.bin.picpro.command
documentation:
Decorator that registers the chosen command/function.

If a function is decorated with @command but that function name is not a valid "command" according to the docstring,
a KeyError will be raised, since that's a bug in this script.

If a user doesn't specify a valid command in their command line arguments, the above docopt(__doc__) line will print
a short summary and call sys.exit() and stop up there.

If a user specifies a valid command, but for some reason the developer did not register it, an AttributeError will
raise, since it is a bug in this script.

Finally, if a user specifies a valid command and it is registered with @command below, then that command is "chosen"
by this decorator function, and set as the attribute `chosen`. It is then executed below in
`if __name__ == '__main__':`.

Positional arguments:
func -- the function to decorate

functions.name: picpro.bin.picpro.programmer_common_bootstrap
documentation:
Given a serial port ID, PIC type, hex file name, and other optional
data, attempt to program the hex file data to a PIC in the programmer.

functions.name: picpro.bin.picpro.program_pic
documentation:
Given a serial port ID, PIC type, hex file name, and other optional
data, attempt to program the hex file data to a PIC in the programmer.
